full_response:
Given  
R_{abcd}= -\dfrac{1}{l^{2}}\,(g_{ac}g_{bd}-g_{ad}g_{bc})                                                (★)  

Step 1:  Find the Ricci tensor  

R_{bd}=R^{a}{}_{bad}=g^{ae}R_{ebad}.  
Insert (★):

R_{bd}= -\frac{1}{l^{2}}\;g^{ae}\!\left(g_{ea}g_{bd}-g_{ed}g_{ba}\right)
      = -\frac{1}{l^{2}}\;\bigl[(g^{ae}g_{ea})\,g_{bd}-(g^{ae}g_{ed})\,g_{ba}\bigr].

• g^{ae}g_{ea}=δ^{a}{}_{a}=N, where N is the spacetime dimension.  
• g^{ae}g_{ed}=δ^{a}{}_{d}.  Contracting with g_{ba} gives g_{bd}.

Hence  
R_{bd}= -\frac{1}{l^{2}}\;(N-1)\,g_{bd}.                                                  (◆)

For our metric N=4, so  
R_{bd}= -\dfrac{3}{l^{2}}\,g_{bd}.

Step 2:  Contract once more for the Ricci scalar  

R=g^{bd}R_{bd}= -\frac{N-1}{l^{2}}\;g^{bd}g_{bd}
  = -\frac{N-1}{l^{2}}\;N
  = -\frac{N(N-1)}{l^{2}}.

With N=4:  

R= -\dfrac{4\cdot3}{l^{2}}= -\dfrac{12}{l^{2}}.

\boxed{R = -\dfrac{12}{l^{2}}}

Confidence: 90%
